description: Named audit lanes for the multi-CLI skill-audit fleet. A lane binds a set of importance bands to one operation, a CAPABILITY TIER floor (minTier), and a concurrency cap. An agent may claim a lane only if its ACTUAL runner model's tier (paths.modelTierName: opus/gpt55/gemini=high, sonnet/gpt54=mid, haiku/gemini-flash/minimax/nemotron=cheap) is >= the lane's minTier. The actual runner model — never a lane-fixed model — is what gets recorded in the lock and ledger, so any qualifying CLI (Claude Opus, GPT-5.5/Codex, Gemini 3.1 Pro) serves a critical lane with honest attribution. `preferredModels` is advisory routing guidance only. Changed 2.0.0 (2026-05-22): replaced the single forcing `model` field with `minTier` + `preferredModels` per the multi-model review (lanes were corrupting attribution by overwriting the runner's model). Relocated 2026-05-25 to skill-graph/audits/lanes.json (project-owned per ADR-0016 surface #1; the previous workspace path .opencode/skill-audit-lanes.json is deleted in the same commit).

critical-audit.rationale: Highest-centrality skills get deep, graded frontier-tier audits — these route the most agents, so an error here propagates widest. Any high-tier CLI may serve it.

high-audit.rationale: High-band skills get graded mid-tier-or-better audits — strong reasoning at a fraction of frontier cost.

bulk-audit.rationale: Low-centrality skills get cheap deterministic + drift audits; any tier may serve, but route the cheapest capable model here.

improve.rationale: Field-sized improvements need reasoning. One field, one commit, time-boxed, keep-or-revert.

bulk-evaluate.rationale: Running a skill's declared eval suite is mechanical execution — route to the cheapest capable model.

merge.rationale: Curator merge of multi-model proposals into one SKILL.md — single serial owner avoids merge races; frontier-tier judgment required.
